### Scaffold Project with Tiged

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/index.md

Example of using tiged to create a local copy of a community template and run it.

```bash
npx tiged user/project my-project
cd my-project

npm install
npm run dev
```

--------------------------------

### Install Vite CLI

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/index.md

Commands to install Vite as a development dependency using various package managers.

```bash
$ npm install -D vite
```

```bash
$ yarn add -D vite
```

```bash
$ pnpm add -D vite
```

```bash
$ bun add -D vite
```

```bash
$ deno add -D npm:vite
```

--------------------------------

### Basic index.html

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/index.md

A simple HTML file for a Vite project.

```html
<p>Hello Vite!</p>
```

--------------------------------

### Run Vite Dev Server

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/index.md

Commands to start the Vite development server using various package managers.

```bash
$ npx vite
```

```bash
$ yarn vite
```

```bash
$ pnpm vite
```

```bash
$ bunx vite
```

```bash
$ deno run -A npm:vite
```

--------------------------------

### Scaffolding Your First Vite Project

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/index.md

Commands to scaffold a new Vite project using different package managers.

```bash
$ npm create vite@latest
```

```bash
$ yarn create vite
```

```bash
$ pnpm create vite
```

```bash
$ bun create vite
```

```bash
$ deno init --npm vite
```

--------------------------------

### Build and link Vite locally

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/index.md

Steps to clone the Vite repository, build it, and link it locally for development, requiring pnpm.

```bash
git clone https://github.com/vitejs/vite.git
cd vite
pnpm install
cd packages/vite
pnpm run build
pnpm link # use your preferred package manager for this step
```

--------------------------------

### Example

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/config/preview-options.md

Example configuration for setting server and preview ports.

```js
export default defineConfig({
  server: {
    port: 3030,
  },
  preview: {
    port: 8080,
  },
})
```

--------------------------------

### server.open configuration example

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/config/server-options.md

Example of configuring `server.open` to automatically open a specific URL path in the browser on server start.

```js
export default defineConfig({
  server: {
    open: '/docs/index.html'
  }
})
```

--------------------------------

### `preview` function example

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/api-javascript.md

Example demonstrating how to use the `preview` function to start a preview server and interact with it.

```ts
import { preview } from 'vite'

const previewServer = await preview({
  // any valid user config options, plus `mode` and `configFile`
  preview: {
    port: 8080,
    open: true,
  },
})

previewServer.printUrls()
previewServer.bindCLIShortcuts({ print: true })
```

--------------------------------

### Default npm Scripts

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/index.md

Standard npm scripts for a Vite project in `package.json`.

```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "vite", // start dev server, aliases: `vite dev`, `vite serve`
    "build": "vite build", // build for production
    "preview": "vite preview" // locally preview production build
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Example Barrel File

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/performance.md

An example of a barrel file re-exporting APIs from other files.

```js
export * from './color.js'
export * from './dom.js'
export * from './slash.js'
```

--------------------------------

### Install specific commit

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/index.md

Commands to install a specific unreleased commit of Vite using various package managers.

```bash
$ npm install -D https://pkg.pr.new/vite@SHA
```

```bash
$ yarn add -D https://pkg.pr.new/vite@SHA
```

```bash
$ pnpm add -D https://pkg.pr.new/vite@SHA
```

```bash
$ bun add -D https://pkg.pr.new/vite@SHA
```

--------------------------------

### Configuring Server Warmup

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/performance.md

Example `vite.config.js` configuration to warm up frequently used client files, preventing request waterfalls.

```js
export default defineConfig({
  server: {
    warmup: {
      clientFiles: [
        './src/components/BigComponent.vue',
        './src/utils/big-utils.js'
      ]
    }
  }
})
```

--------------------------------

### Netlify CLI Installation

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/static-deploy.md

Command to install the Netlify CLI globally.

```bash
npm install -g netlify-cli
```

--------------------------------

### Named Imports with setup

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/features.md

Example of importing specific parts of modules using the 'import' option.

```ts twoslash
import 'vite/client'
// ---cut---
const modules = import.meta.glob('./dir/*.js', { import: 'setup' })
```

```ts
// code produced by vite
const modules = {
  './dir/bar.js': () => import('./dir/bar.js').then((m) => m.setup),
  './dir/foo.js': () => import('./dir/foo.js').then((m) => m.setup),
}
```

--------------------------------

### Inspecting Transform Logs

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/performance.md

Example output from running `vite --debug transform` to identify frequently used files and their transformation times.

```bash
vite:transform 28.72ms /@vite/client +1ms
vite:transform 62.95ms /src/components/BigComponent.vue +1ms
vite:transform 102.54ms /src/utils/big-utils.js +1ms
```

--------------------------------

### Example

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/config/shared-options.md

Example of defining global constant replacements in Vite configuration.

```js
export default defineConfig({
  define: {
    __APP_VERSION__: JSON.stringify('v1.0.0'),
    __API_URL__: 'window.__backend_api_url',
  },
})
```

--------------------------------

### Example of build.modulePreload.resolveDependencies

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/config/build-options.md

Configuration example showing how to use the `resolveDependencies` function to filter module preload dependencies.

```js
/** @type {import('vite').UserConfig} */
const config = {
  // prettier-ignore
  build: {
// ---cut-before---
modulePreload: {
  resolveDependencies: (filename, deps, { hostId, hostType }) => {
    return deps.filter(condition)
  },
},
// ---cut-after---
  },
}
```

--------------------------------

### Install dependencies

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/packages/create-vite/template-qwik/README.md

Command to install project dependencies using npm, pnpm, or yarn.

```bash
$ npm install # or pnpm install or yarn install
```

--------------------------------

### Example License Metadata JSON

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/config/build-options.md

An example of the JSON metadata generated when `build.license.fileName` ends with `.json`.

```json
[
  {
    "name": "dep-1",
    "version": "1.2.3",
    "identifier": "CC0-1.0",
    "text": "CC0 1.0 Universal\n\n..."
  },
  {
    "name": "dep-2",
    "version": "4.5.6",
    "identifier": "MIT",
    "text": "MIT License\n\n..."
  }
]
```

--------------------------------

### CSS Styling Example

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/playground/worker/index.html

Example of basic CSS styling for paragraphs and a class.

```css
p { background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1); }
.classname { color: green; }
```

--------------------------------

### createServer Example Usage

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/api-javascript.md

Example usage of `createServer` with inline configuration options.

```ts
import { createServer } from 'vite'

const server = await createServer({
  // any valid user config options, plus `mode` and `configFile`
  configFile: false,
  root: import.meta.dirname,
  server: {
    port: 1337,
  },
})
await server.listen()

server.printUrls()
server.bindCLIShortcuts({ print: true })
```

--------------------------------

### Install SWC Plugin for Decorators

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/migration.md

Commands to install the SWC plugin and core for lowering native decorators.

```bash
$ npm install -D @rollup/plugin-swc @swc/core
```

```bash
$ yarn add -D @rollup/plugin-swc @swc/core
```

```bash
$ pnpm add -D @rollup/plugin-swc @swc/core
```

```bash
$ bun add -D @rollup/plugin-swc @swc/core
```

```bash
$ deno add -D npm:@rollup/plugin-swc npm:@swc/core
```

--------------------------------

### Custom Logger Implementation Example

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/config/shared-options.md

Example demonstrating how to create and customize a logger to filter out specific warnings.

```ts
import { createLogger, defineConfig } from 'vite'

const logger = createLogger()
const loggerWarn = logger.warn

logger.warn = (msg, options) => {
  // Ignore empty CSS files warning
  if (msg.includes('vite:css') && msg.includes(' is empty')) return
  loggerWarn(msg, options)
}

export default defineConfig({
  customLogger: logger,
})
```

--------------------------------

### Vercel CLI Installation and Deployment

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/static-deploy.md

Command to install the Vercel CLI globally and deploy an application.

```bash
npm i -g vercel
```

```bash
vercel
```

--------------------------------

### Example Tags for views/foo.js Entry Point

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/backend-integration.md

HTML tags that should be included in production for the entry point `views/foo.js`, based on the example manifest.

```html
<link rel="stylesheet" href="assets/foo-5UjPuW-k.css" />
<link rel="stylesheet" href="assets/shared-ChJ_j-JJ.css" />
<script type="module" src="assets/foo-BRBmoGS9.js"></script>
<!-- optional -->
<link rel="modulepreload" href="assets/shared-B7PI925R.js" />
```

--------------------------------

### Install Babel Plugin for Decorators

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/migration.md

Commands to install the Babel plugin and decorator proposal plugin for lowering native decorators.

```bash
$ npm install -D @rolldown/plugin-babel @babel/plugin-proposal-decorators
```

```bash
$ yarn add -D @rolldown/plugin-babel @babel/plugin-proposal-decorators
```

```bash
$ pnpm add -D @rolldown/plugin-babel @babel/plugin-proposal-decorators
```

```bash
$ bun add -D @rolldown/plugin-babel @babel/plugin-proposal-decorators
```

```bash
$ deno add -D npm:@rolldown/plugin-babel npm:@babel/plugin-proposal-decorators
```

--------------------------------

### configurePreviewServer

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/api-plugin.md

Hook to configure the preview server, similar to `configureServer`. It allows injecting custom middleware before or after internal middlewares are installed.

```APIDOC
## configurePreviewServer

### Description
Hook to configure the preview server, similar to `configureServer`. It allows injecting custom middleware before or after internal middlewares are installed. This hook is called before other middlewares are installed. If a function is returned, it will be called after internal middlewares are installed.

### Method
N/A (Plugin Hook)

### Endpoint
N/A

### Parameters
- **server** (PreviewServer) - The preview server instance.

### Returns
- `(() => void) | void | Promise<(() => void) | void>` - A function to be called after internal middlewares, or void.

### Request Example
```js
const myPlugin = () => ({
  name: 'configure-preview-server',
  configurePreviewServer(server) {
    // return a post hook that is called after other middlewares are
    // installed
    return () => {
      server.middlewares.use((req, res, next) => {
        // custom handle request...
      })
    }
  },
})
```
```

--------------------------------

### Classic Worker Instantiation

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/playground/worker/index.html

Instantiating a classic worker, for the .classic-worker test case.

```javascript
new Worker(new URL('./classic-worker.js', import.meta.url))
```

--------------------------------

### Importing an Inline Worker

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/playground/worker/index.html

Importing an inline worker module for the .pong-inline test case.

```javascript
import InlineWorker from '../my-worker?worker&inline'
```

--------------------------------

### Classic Shared Worker Instantiation

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/playground/worker/index.html

Instantiating a classic shared worker, for the .classic-shared-worker test case.

```javascript
new SharedWorker(new URL('./classic-shared-worker.js', import.meta.url), { type: 'classic' })
```

--------------------------------

### Importing and Instantiating a Named Worker

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/playground/worker/index.html

Importing and instantiating a worker module with a specific name, for the .pong-named test case.

```javascript
import myWorker from '../my-worker?worker' new myWorker({ name: "named-worker" })
```

--------------------------------

### Importing an Inline Shared Worker

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/playground/worker/index.html

Importing an inline shared worker module for the .pong-shared-inline test case.

```javascript
import InlineSharedWorker from '../my-shared-worker?sharedworker&inline'
```

--------------------------------

### Dynamic Asset Loading with import.meta.url

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/playground/assets/index.html

Examples demonstrating various ways to dynamically load assets using `new URL(path, import.meta.url)`, including paths with queries, ternary operators, and different file types. It also includes a helper function for displaying results.

```javascript
function testDynamicImportMetaUrl(name, i) {
  const metaUrl = new URL(`./nested/${name}.png`, import.meta.url)
  text(`.dynamic-import-meta-url-${i}`, metaUrl)
  document.querySelector(`.dynamic-import-meta-url-img-${i}`).src = metaUrl
}
testDynamicImportMetaUrl('icon', 1)
testDynamicImportMetaUrl('asset', 2)

function testDynamicImportMetaUrlWithComma(name, i) {
  // prettier-ignore
  const metaUrl = new URL(`./nested/${name}.png`, import.meta.url,)
  text(`.dynamic-import-meta-url-${i}-comma`, metaUrl)
  document.querySelector(`.dynamic-import-meta-url-img-${i}-comma`).src = metaUrl
}
testDynamicImportMetaUrlWithComma('icon', 1)
testDynamicImportMetaUrlWithComma('asset', 2)

function testDynamicImportMetaUrlWithQuery(name, i) {
  // prettier-ignore
  const metaUrl = new URL(`./nested/${name}.png?abc`, import.meta.url,)
  text(`.dynamic-import-meta-url-${i}-query`, metaUrl)
  document.querySelector(`.dynamic-import-meta-url-img-${i}-query`).src = metaUrl
}
testDynamicImportMetaUrlWithQuery('icon', 1)
testDynamicImportMetaUrlWithQuery('asset', 2)

function testDynamicImportMetaUrlWithTernaryOperator(name, i) {
  // prettier-ignore
  const metaUrl = new URL(`./nested/${1 === 0 ? 'failed' : name}.png?abc`, import.meta.url,)
  text(`.dynamic-import-meta-url-${i}-ternary`, metaUrl)
  document.querySelector(`.dynamic-import-meta-url-img-${i}-ternary`).src = metaUrl
}
testDynamicImportMetaUrlWithTernaryOperator('icon', 1)
testDynamicImportMetaUrlWithTernaryOperator('asset', 2)

{
  const name = 'test'
  const js = new URL(`./nested/${name}.js`, import.meta.url).href
  text('.dynamic-import-meta-url-js', js)
}

{
  const name = './nested/icon'
  const metaUrl = new URL(`${name}.png`, import.meta.url)
  text(`.dynamic-import-meta-url-all`, metaUrl)
}

text('.import-meta-url-multiline', multilineUrl)

function text(el, text) {
  document.querySelector(el).textContent = text
}
```

--------------------------------

### Nested Worker with import.meta.url

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/playground/worker/index.html

Importing a nested worker and testing import.meta.url behavior, for the .nested-worker test case.

```javascript
import NestedWorker from './worker-nested-worker?worker'
```

--------------------------------

### Injecting Post-Middleware for Vite Preview Server

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/api-plugin.md

Use this snippet to inject custom middleware that runs after Vite's internal middlewares are installed in the preview server. The `configurePreviewServer` hook returns a function that executes post-installation.

```js
const myPlugin = () => ({
  name: 'configure-preview-server',
  configurePreviewServer(server) {
    // return a post hook that is called after other middlewares are
    // installed
    return () => {
      server.middlewares.use((req, res, next) => {
        // custom handle request...
      })
    }
  }
})
```

--------------------------------

### Importing and Instantiating a Named Inline Worker

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/playground/worker/index.html

Importing and instantiating an inline worker module with a specific name, for the .pong-inline-named test case.

```javascript
import InlineWorker from '../my-worker?worker&inline' new InlineWorker({ name: "named-inline-worker" })
```

--------------------------------

### Inline Worker with import.meta.url Access

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/playground/worker/index.html

Importing and instantiating an inline worker, demonstrating access to import.meta.url, for the .pong-inline-url test case.

```javascript
import InlineWorker from '../my-worker?worker&inline' new InlineWorker() import.meta.url
```

--------------------------------

### Module Worker with URL and import.meta.url

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/playground/worker/index.html

Instantiating a module worker using a URL relative to import.meta.url, for the .worker-import-meta-url test case.

```javascript
new Worker(new URL('./url-worker.js', import.meta.url), { type: 'module' })
```

--------------------------------

### Importing a Shared Worker

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/playground/worker/index.html

Importing a named shared worker module for the .tick-count test case.

```javascript
import mySharedWorker from '../my-shared-worker?sharedworker&name=shared'
```

--------------------------------

### Deeply Nested Module Worker

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/playground/worker/index.html

Instantiating a deeply nested module worker, for the .deeply-nested-worker test case.

```javascript
new Worker(new URL('../deeply-nested-worker.js', import.meta.url), { type: 'module' })
```

--------------------------------

### Importing and Instantiating a Named Shared Worker

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/playground/worker/index.html

Importing and instantiating a named shared worker module, for the .tick-count-named test case.

```javascript
import mySharedWorker from '../my-shared-worker?sharedworker&name=shared' new mySharedWorker({ name: "namedSharedWorker" })
```

--------------------------------

### Module Worker with URL Without Extension

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/playground/worker/index.html

Instantiating a module worker using a URL without a file extension and import.meta.url, for the .worker-import-meta-url-without-extension test case.

```javascript
new Worker(new URL('./url-worker', import.meta.url), { type: 'module' })
```

--------------------------------

### Asset Import and Usage Examples

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/playground/assets/index.html

This comprehensive snippet showcases diverse asset import strategies in a JavaScript context, covering CSS imports, image imports (relative, absolute, public, inline), JSON and JavaScript file imports as URLs, SVG fragment imports, raw asset imports, and usage of `import.meta.url` for dynamic asset resolution.

```javascript
@import '/foo.css'; import './css/fonts.css' import './css/css-url.css' import './css/icons.css' import './css/manual-chunks.css' import { multilineUrl } from './multiline-import-meta-url.js' text('.base', `import.meta.${''}env.BASE_URL: ${import.meta.env.BASE_URL}`) import url from './nested/asset.png' text('.asset-import-relative', url) import absoluteUrl from '/nested/asset.png' text('.asset-import-absolute', absoluteUrl) import publicUrl from '/icon.png' text('.public-import', publicUrl) import publicJsonUrl from '/foo.json?url' text('.public-json-import', publicJsonUrl) ;(async () => { const res = await fetch(publicJsonUrl) text('.public-json-import-content', await res.text()) })() import publicJsUrl from '/raw.js?url' text('.public-js-import', publicJsUrl) ;(async () => { const res = await fetch(publicJsUrl) text('.public-js-import-content', await res.text()) text( '.public-js-import-content-type', await res.headers.get('Content-Type'), ) })() import publicTsUrl from '/raw.ts?url' text('.public-ts-import', publicTsUrl) ;(async () => { const res = await fetch(publicTsUrl) text('.public-ts-import-content', await res.text()) text( '.public-ts-import-content-type', await res.headers.get('Content-Type'), ) })() import publicMtsUrl from '/raw.mts?url' text('.public-mts-import', publicMtsUrl) ;(async () => { const res = await fetch(publicMtsUrl) text('.public-mts-import-content', await res.text()) text( '.public-mts-import-content-type', await res.headers.get('Content-Type'), ) })() import svgFrag from './nested/fragment.svg' text('.svg-frag-import-path', svgFrag) document.querySelector('.svg-frag-import').src = svgFrag + '#icon-heart-view' import unknownExtUrl from './nested/foo.unknown' text('.unknown-ext', unknownExtUrl) import rawSvg from './nested/fragment.svg?raw' text('.raw', rawSvg) import rawHtml from './nested/partial.html?raw' text('.raw-html', rawHtml) import.meta.hot?.accept('./nested/partial.html?raw', (m) => { text('.raw-html', m.default) }) import noInlineSvg from './nested/fragment.svg?no-inline' text('.no-inline-svg', noInlineSvg) import noInlineSvgMP from './nested/fragment.svg?no-inline&foo=bar' text('.no-inline-svg-mp', noInlineSvgMP) import inlinePng from './nested/asset.png?inline' text('.inline-png', inlinePng) import inlinePublicPng from '/icon.png?inline' text('.inline-public-png', inlinePublicPng) import inlinePublicJson from '/foo.json?url&inline' text('.inline-public-json', inlinePublicJson) import fooUrl from './foo.js?url' text('.url', fooUrl) import unicodeUrl from './テスト-測試-white space.js?url' text('.unicode-url', unicodeUrl) import filenameIncludingSingleQuoteUrl from "./nested/with-single'quote.png" text('.filename-including-single-quote', filenameIncludingSingleQuoteUrl) // TODO: is not supported yet (https://github.com/vitejs/vite/pull/16243) // import percentUrl from './asset/percent%25.png?url' // text('.percent-url', percentUrl) import cssUrl from './css/icons.css?url' text('.url-css', cssUrl) import cssUrlUrl from './css/css-url-url.css?url' const linkTag = document.createElement('link') linkTag.href = cssUrlUrl linkTag.rel = 'stylesheet' document.body.appendChild(linkTag) // const url = new URL('non_existent_file.png', import.meta.url) const metaUrl = new URL('./import-meta-url/img.png', import.meta.url) text('.import-meta-url', metaUrl) document.querySelector('.import-meta-url-img').src = metaUrl const metaUrlDep = new URL('@/asset.png', import.meta.url) text('.import-meta-url-dep', metaUrlDep) document.querySelector('.import-meta-url-dep-img').src = metaUrlDep // testing URLs for public assets served at the public base path // equivalent to `new URL(`${import.meta.env.BASE_URL}/icon.png`, import.meta.url) const metaUrlBasePath = new URL('/icon.png', import.meta.url) text('.import-meta-url-base-path', metaUrlBasePath) document.querySelector('.import-meta-url-base-path-img').src = metaUrlBasePath const metaUrlDataUri = new URL( 'data:image/png;base64,
```

--------------------------------

### Shared Module Worker with URL and import.meta.url

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/playground/worker/index.html

Instantiating a module shared worker using a URL relative to import.meta.url, for the .shared-worker-import-meta-url test case.

```javascript
new SharedWorker(new URL('./url-shared-worker.js', import.meta.url), { type: 'module' })
```

--------------------------------

### Worker with URL .href

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/playground/worker/index.html

Instantiating a worker using the .href property of a URL derived from import.meta.url, for the .simple-worker-url test case.

```javascript
new Worker(new URL('../simple-worker.js', import.meta.url).href)
```

--------------------------------

### Configure preview port

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/guide/static-deploy.md

Example of configuring the preview server's port in package.json.

```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "preview": "vite preview --port 8080"
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Require JSON in Worker

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/playground/worker/index.html

Demonstrates requiring a JSON file within a worker, for the .worker-require-json test case.

```javascript
require('./some.json') in worker
```

--------------------------------

### server.proxy configuration example

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/docs/config/server-options.md

Demonstrates various ways to configure proxy rules, including string shorthand, options object, RegExp matching, using the proxy instance, and proxying WebSockets.

```js
export default defineConfig({
  server: {
    proxy: {
      // string shorthand:
      // http://localhost:5173/foo
      //   -> http://localhost:4567/foo
      '/foo': 'http://localhost:4567',
      // with options:
      // http://localhost:5173/api/bar
      //   -> http://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/bar
      '/api': {
        target: 'http://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com',
        changeOrigin: true,
        rewrite: (path) => path.replace(/^\/api/, ''),
      },
      // with RegExp:
      // http://localhost:5173/fallback/
      //   -> http://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/
      '^/fallback/.*': {
        target: 'http://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com',
        changeOrigin: true,
        rewrite: (path) => path.replace(/^\/fallback/, ''),
      },
      // Using the proxy instance
      '/api': {
        target: 'http://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com',
        changeOrigin: true,
        configure: (proxy, options) => {
          // proxy will be an instance of 'http-proxy'
        },
      },
      // Proxying websockets or socket.io:
      // ws://localhost:5173/socket.io
      //   -> ws://localhost:5174/socket.io
      // Exercise caution using `rewriteWsOrigin` as it can leave the
      // proxying open to CSRF attacks.
      '/socket.io': {
        target: 'ws://localhost:5174',
        ws: true,
        rewriteWsOrigin: true,
      },
    },
  },
})
```

--------------------------------

### Module Worker with URL Alias Resolution

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/playground/worker/index.html

Instantiating a module worker using a URL with an alias and import.meta.url for resolution, for the .worker-import-meta-url-resolve test case.

```javascript
new Worker(new URL('@/url-worker', import.meta.url), { type: 'module' })
```

--------------------------------

### Worker from TypeScript Output

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/playground/worker/index.html

Importing a worker module that might be a TypeScript output, for the .pong-ts-output test case.

```javascript
import TSOutputWorker from '../possible-ts-output-worker?worker'
```

--------------------------------

### Second Deeply Nested Module Worker

Source: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/blob/main/playground/worker/index.html

Instantiating a second deeply nested module worker, for the .deeply-nested-second-worker test case.

```javascript
new Worker(new URL('deeply-nested-second-worker.js', import.meta.url), { type: 'module' })
```
